Welcome back. I want to now break down a few of our examples on the moment of highest pleasure. The one thing I really want you to pay attention to here is what we spoke about before, how it has more to do with redemption and correcting an injustice than it does approaching something in more of an aspirational way. Just look at the language and try to notice those patterns and get them kind of going in your own mind so that when you write your own moment of highest pleasure you can be more of that mindset of redemption and overcoming injustice than aspiration, which is really the biggest mistake people make in describing pleasure. The first one is back with our friend Greg Hickman and his program Funnels at Scale. So headline, with all that intense effort and commitment, isn't a time you actually started receiving some damn payoff. So even right from the headline you see that this is about cashing in on that intense effort and commitment and then doing that wrong. Start receiving some damn payoff. So you can almost have this type of headline for any type of product and it would still work. The point here isn't to have big very general headlines, but the point I'm trying to make here is that your headline right from the start can point towards that implied injustice. So with the amount of time, effort and bullish commitment you've invested in growing your online business through automated marketing funnels, you deserve the payoff. Qualifying what makes them deserve here. Not just saying you deserve to be happy. Why do they deserve to be happy? Why do they deserve that payoff? So here you're linking that to all the effort and commitment they've already showed. You deserve to know exactly how to fully leverage your time and energy. Waking up every morning to a steady stream of Sam cart or PayPal notifications and a crystal clear idea of what's needed to amplify your results. So here you have both a very tangible outcome, a very vivid tangible one waking up to PayPal notifications, as well as the emotional one, having that crystal clear idea and all that confidence around what they need to do to amplify it. You deserve to actually have the bulk of the work done for you instead of being told what to do and then being funnel shamed by coaches who make a living telling people what to do yet never doing it themselves. So pointing towards a common enemy there, which we uncovered in the 67 questions. You deserve to actually feel like the powerful leader and CEO of a quickly growing online business instead of a perpetual seeker jumping from one course to the next, hoping this will be the one in a moment. I'm going to show you how to finally tip the scales back in your favor. But first, a few key things you got to soak your brains gray mad gray matter into. And then we go into the sacred cows and reengineering the beliefs that line up with his program. The second example here is with our friends at indestructible team. And the language here isn't the same as funnels that scale and rightfully so different audiences, different 67 questions process, but you'll see the same theme here. It is all about the implied injustice headline, the secret to working on your business instead of in it. So mirroring back language. They're very familiar with here. Let's be real. You don't need or even want a Tim Ferriss approved for our work week. You just want to be able to work on your business, grow your income and fulfill your company's mission without sacrificing the best years of your life to do it. So it's almost a very modest aspiration, right? You're kind of taking it down from that aspiration, that four hour work week and almost vilifying that aspiration and making it a lot more grounded. You know, you just want to work on your business, grow your income. Is that too much to ask, you know, without sacrificing the best years of your life? So bringing it down to that level of physical safety on that whole Maslow's pyramid. You want to be as involved in the day today as you choose to be. And at this point in your career, so there's some exclusive empowerment going on there, you feel like you've earned the right, earned the right injustice to put your phone on airplane mode, sit down for a decent meal and spoil yourself with a long shower without mentally triaging through a never ending to do list. Spoiler yourself with a shower like once again, not crazy aspirational, but something that based on who they are and what they've accomplished, they should be able to do. And then we continue self care shouldn't be this mythical thing with no basis in your reality. I have no time shouldn't be the most frequently thing uttered from your lips and late night venting to your partner should, there's a word should be replaced with clink worthy winds celebrating the predictable, trackable and exponential growth that your business generates every single day without you doing all the nitty gritty. So one thing you'll notice with this example is that you'll see a lot of the same elements as the moment of highest tension, except we're framing it as the removal of those things. So you could think of that moment of highest pleasure as the removal of all those injustices and all the symptoms that that injustice includes. So I think you have enough examples in front of you. You've seen the lesson you've done the work you've done module two to really gather all the insights on this. Let's take a breather and then take a crack at writing your moment of highest pleasure.
